Menu

ምናሌ

This is a sample menu that is updated frequently. Some dishes and ingredients will change with the season.

To Begin መነሻ

Sambusa ሳምቡሳ9
Crisp pastry, spiced lentils or minced beef
Azifa አዚፋ10
Green lentil salad, mustard, lemon
Timatim Fitfit ጥማጥም11
Torn injera, tomato, onion, green chili
Ye'shimbra Asa ሽምብራ10
Chickpea-flour "fish," berbere, fried golden

From the Fire ዋና ምግቦች

Doro Wat ዶሮ ወጥ22
Slow-braised chicken, berbere, soft egg
Tibs ጥብስ21
Seared beef or lamb, rosemary, awaze
Kitfo ክትፎ23
Minced beef, mitmita, niter kibbeh
Yebeg Alicha የበግ አልጫ22
Mild turmeric lamb, ginger, garlic

Fasting Table የጾም ምግብ

Beyaynetu በያይነቱ19
Platter of the day's vegetable wots & greens
Misir Wat ምስር ወጥ14
Red lentils simmered slow in berbere
Shiro ሽሮ14
Silky ground-chickpea stew, garlic
Gomen ጎመን13
Collard greens, ginger, garlic

Drinks & Coffee መጠጥ

Buna Ceremony ቡና16
Beans roasted at the table, for two
Spris ስፕሪስ8
Layered avocado, mango & papaya juice
Tej ጠጅ10
House honey wine, lightly sparkling
Telba & Tea ተልባ6
Flax drink or spiced Ethiopian tea

Many dishes are naturally vegan and gluten-free.
Please tell us about any allergies — we're glad to help.

About

ስለ እኛ

Adey Cafe is a neighborhood Ethiopian kitchen in West Philadelphia. We simmer our wots for hours, ferment our injera for days, and blend our berbere by hand. Everything arrives in the middle of the table — injera takes the place of the fork, and the finest bite, gursha, is the one you offer someone else. Pull up a chair, stay a while, and let the coffee ceremony close the night.

Kitfo
Tibs from the Skillet
Doro Wat & Injera
